ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for pandan-flavoured bread with chocolate spread; mapped to the nearest parent class 'bread food product' (FOODON_03315595) as an approximate anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for pandan bread with chocolate spread; A000E (Bread and similar products) is the closest defensible category anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for pandan bread with chocolate spread; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is approximately 2 slices of pandan bread (about 80 g) spread with a standard portion of chocolate hazelnut spread, commonly consumed at breakfast or as a snack in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Pandan bread with chocolate spread — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for pandan bread with chocolate spread (cr_id F05020074). All per-100g values are taken as reported. Saturated fat is not separately reported by HPB for this entry; value estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous entry for chocolate hazelnut spread on white bread (USDA SR Legacy) and documented accordingly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — Atkinson et al. 2008; University of Sydney GI Database — white bread analogous estimate for enriched pandan bread
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for pandan bread with chocolate spread in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 40 is an analogous estimate based on enriched white bread with added fibre (GI ~40–45 range per Sydney GI Database for high-fibre white bread variants). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (40) × available carbohydrate per serving (47.79 g/100g × 80 g serving = 38.2 g) / 100 = 15.3. The presence of chocolate hazelnut spread (high sugar, fat) may modestly lower the GI via fat-mediated gastric emptying delay, consistent with the low-GI estimate. Input record cited GI 40 with confidence 0.397; this analogous estimate is consistent with that value and is retained with analogous_estimate classification.

hypertension.key_factor: Elevated sodium (270 mg/100g), high sugar (11.8 g/100g), and low potassium (74 mg/100g) create an unfavourable sodium-to-potassium ratio that may elevate blood pressure.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one slice and avoid pairing with other high-sodium foods, as the 270 mg sodium per 100 g combined with low potassium (74 mg/100g) and high sugar (11.8 g/100g) creates a clinically unfavourable cardiovascular load; substituting with a low-sugar, unsweetened spread and wholemeal pandan bread would improve the nutrient profile.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High total carbohydrate (47.79 g/100g) with significant added sugar (11.8 g/100g) from chocolate hazelnut spread elevates postprandial glucose risk despite moderate dietary fibre (3.7 g/100g).
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this as a high-carbohydrate food requiring careful portion control (maximum one slice per sitting), pair it with a protein source to blunt the glycaemic response, and consider substituting the chocolate hazelnut spread with an unsweetened nut butter to reduce the added sugar load from 11.8 g/100g.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Chocolate hazelnut spread typically contains palm oil (saturated fat) and high sugar; combined with 270 mg sodium/100g and low potassium, this food presents a moderate cardiovascular risk profile.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to one slice occasionally, choose a chocolate spread formulated without palm oil or with reduced saturated fat, and prioritise wholemeal pandan bread to benefit from the higher fibre content, as the estimated saturated fat (~2.8 g/100g) and sodium (270 mg/100g) together contribute meaningfully to daily cardiovascular risk thresholds.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Palm oil in chocolate hazelnut spread is pro-inflammatory; sugar at 11.8 g/100g approaches the RA refined-sugar threshold.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should verify the chocolate spread's ingredient list for palm oil and, if present, substitute with unsweetened almond butter or tahini to achieve an anti-inflammatory fat profile, as palm oil is excluded from the MUFA/PUFA anti-inflammatory carve-out.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pandan bread with chocolate spread is a popular Singaporean breakfast and snack item consisting of sliced pandan-flavoured white bread spread with a chocolate hazelnut spread such as Nutella or a similar product. Pandan (Pandanus amaryllifolius) is a tropical plant whose leaves impart a distinctive green colour and a sweet, vanilla-like fragrance to baked goods; pandan bread is a staple in Singapore's bakeries and kopitiams (traditional coffee shops). The bread is soft, moist, and mildly sweet with a characteristic pandan aroma, while the chocolate spread adds a rich, sweet, and slightly nutty flavour. Per 100 g, this food provides 309 kcal, 11.19 g protein, 8.33 g fat, 47.79 g carbohydrate, 11.8 g sugar, 3.7 g dietary fibre, 270 mg sodium, 300 mg calcium, and 4.18 mg iron. The calcium content is notably high, likely due to fortification of the bread. The dietary fibre content (3.7 g/100g) is moderate and beneficial, but the high sugar from the chocolate spread (11.8 g/100g) and the total carbohydrate load (47.79 g/100g) make this a food requiring portion awareness for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 40 (low GI range), though the high sugar content of the spread may elevate postprandial glucose response in practice. This food is commonly consumed at breakfast or as an afternoon snack, often paired with kopi (local coffee) or Milo. It is suitable for vegetarians and is generally considered halal. Allergens include gluten, tree nuts (hazelnut), soy, and milk derivatives from the chocolate spread.
common_user_queries: Is pandan bread with chocolate spread healthy?
How many calories in pandan bread with Nutella?
Can diabetics eat pandan bread with chocolate spread?
What is the GI of pandan bread?
Is pandan bread with chocolate spread suitable for hypertension?
How much sugar is in pandan bread with chocolate spread?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05020074) with confidence 0.95 as reported in the input record. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogous entry and documented. GI is an analogous estimate consistent with the input record's cited value of 40. Multilingual names are standard translations verified against common usage in Singapore.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all set to -1 because detailed fatty acid fractions, full micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific pandan bread with chocolate spread composite are not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05020074) or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026; both sources were consulted and confirmed unavailability of these disaggregated values for this composite food. Saturated fat (2.8 g/100g) is an analogous estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for chocolate hazelnut spread on enriched white bread; this value is not directly reported by HPB for this entry and should be updated when HPB publishes a disaggregated fatty acid profile. GI of 40 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for high-fibre enriched white bread variants; no direct GI measurement for pandan bread with chocolate spread was found in the Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving (80 g) = 15.3. Calcium at 300 mg/100g is notably high and likely reflects bread fortification; this value is taken as reported from HPB. Cholesterol at 0.34 mg/100g is a near-zero value as reported by HPB; this is plausible given the minimal egg or dairy content in the bread formulation and the low cholesterol content of chocolate hazelnut spread. Halal status is assumed based on common commercial pandan bread and chocolate spread products available in Singapore; individual product verification is recommended.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (3.7), sodium_mg (270.0), saturated_fat_g (2.8, analogous estimate documented), sugar_g (11.8) all present as numeric values with plausible magnitudes.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (40), gi_category (low), glycemic_load_per_serving (15.3), gi_source all present. GI sourced from University of Sydney GI Database as analogous estimate; estimation basis fully documented.

All strings in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array. Self-check passed.
